title: The typeof() and length() functions fail in some virtual tables.
description:
drh added on 2018-09-27 20:04:49:
When the typeof() or length() functions are used on a column of a
virtual table that makes use of the sqlite3_vtab_nochange() optimization,
those functions always return NULL rather than the appropriate value.
bug_features_seed: SELECT CASE WHEN content IS NOT NULL THEN 'TEXT' ELSE NULL END AS content_type FROM test_vtab;
SELECT CASE WHEN content = 'Hello, world!' THEN LENGTH(content) ELSE NULL END AS content_length FROM test_vtab;
SELECT CASE WHEN NULL IS NULL THEN 'NULL' ELSE NULL END AS null_type, LENGTH(NULL) AS null_length;
key_bug_features: typeof() and length() functions return NULL in virtual tables
